levels.label: Exposed
levels.copy: The Cybersecurity & Privacy Readiness pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Basic Hygiene
levels.copy: Some Cybersecurity & Privacy Readiness practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Managed
levels.copy: Core Cybersecurity & Privacy Readiness practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Resilient
levels.copy: Cybersecurity & Privacy Readiness practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Trust-Grade
levels.copy: Cybersecurity & Privacy Readiness is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.govern-and-identify: Assign executive ownership and complete a risk-ranked map of assets, data, identities, and critical suppliers.
recommendations.protect: Close MFA, privileged access, patching, encryption, and immutable-backup gaps for critical services.
recommendations.detect-and-respond: Run a cross-functional incident exercise with decision, evidence, legal, privacy, and communication paths.
recommendations.recover-and-privacy: Test a full restore and operationalise data rights, retention, and privacy impact review.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
